message:
test(fj-cache): clearer empty-fixture message in golden_keys truncation regression

verify_golden_keys_rejects_truncated_snapshots ran
`golden.pop().expect("fixture should contain multiple refs")` to
remove the last entry. If capture_golden_keys() ever returned an
empty Vec the .expect() panic would fire with the slightly
misleading "should contain multiple refs" message — the test was
never written for an empty input either, but a fixture with
exactly one entry would still satisfy "multiple refs" loosely.

Tighten to:

  assert!(!golden.is_empty(), "fixture should contain at least one golden ref");
  let removed = golden.remove(golden.len() - 1);

Two upsides:
- The assert! message is precise about the contract under test
  (we only need at least one entry to demonstrate truncation).
- `golden.remove(golden.len() - 1)` is exactly what `pop()` did
  but without the Option-unwrap dance, so the failure mode if
  the fixture is somehow empty is the assert!, not a generic
  Option panic.

No coverage change — the test still pops the last entry and
expects verify_golden_keys to report a count mismatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
